description:
You are a Linux systems engineer maintaining the “hardener” configuration toolkit that lives in
/home/user/hardener.  
Before shipping the next release you must perform a MINOR semantic-version
bump (e.g. 1.4.2 → 1.5.0) and create a matching changelog entry.

Folder structure that already exists when you begin:

/home/user/hardener
├── version.txt          ← contains only the current version number (no newline at end)
/home/user/hardener/CHANGELOG.md
├── harden-ssh.conf
└── harden-sysctl.conf

Your tasks:

1. Read the current version that is stored in /home/user/hardener/version.txt.  
   • It always follows semantic-versioning MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH.

2. Produce the next MINOR version (increment MINOR, set PATCH to 0).  
   Example: if version.txt is “2.7.3” the next MINOR version is “2.8.0”.
   You do NOT need to verify Git tags; just bump the file.

3. Overwrite /home/user/hardener/version.txt so that it contains **exactly** the
new version string without any extra characters or trailing newline.

4. Prepend a new section to /home/user/hardener/CHANGELOG.md in the precise
format shown below.  The automated checker is strict about spacing and headers,
so follow it literally:

    ## [NEW_VERSION] ‑ YYYY-MM-DD
    ### Added
    - _None_

    ### Changed
    - _None_

    ### Fixed
    - _None_

Substitutions:
• NEW_VERSION  → the version you wrote to version.txt  
• YYYY-MM-DD   → today’s date in ISO 8601 format (UTC)

The entry must be placed at the very top of CHANGELOG.md, **above** the existing
entries, separated from the previous first line by one blank line.

5. Produce a command log: after all work is finished, write a file
/home/user/hardener/bump.log that contains every shell command you issued,
one per line, in the exact order executed.  Do not include comments or prompt
symbols; just the raw commands.  At least 5 and at most 10 commands must be
listed so plan your actions accordingly.

Verification details the test suite will check:
• /home/user/hardener/version.txt holds the correct bumped version string
  with no newline.
• /home/user/hardener/CHANGELOG.md starts with the new header block exactly as
  specified and the remainder of the file is intact below.
• /home/user/hardener/bump.log exists, is UTF-8 text, contains 5-10 lines, and
  every line is a syntactically valid shell command.

Hints:
• You can use command substitution to obtain today’s date.
• Use sed or printf to control newline behaviour when editing version.txt.
• Make sure bump.log includes the command you used to edit each file.
truth:
Initial filesystem state before the agent starts:

1. /home/user/hardener/version.txt
   Content: 1.4.2      (no trailing newline)

2. /home/user/hardener/CHANGELOG.md
   Content:
   ----------------------------------------------------
   ## [1.4.2] - 2023-08-07
   ### Added
   - Initial iptables hardening rules.

   ### Changed
   - Tuned /proc sysctl defaults.

   ### Fixed
   - Corrected typo in sshd_config example.
   ----------------------------------------------------

3. /home/user/hardener/harden-ssh.conf
   (some example SSH configuration, irrelevant to task)

4. /home/user/hardener/harden-sysctl.conf
   (some sysctl hardening settings, irrelevant to task)

Target final state that the autograder will validate:

A. /home/user/hardener/version.txt
   Exact content: 1.5.0   (no newline, three characters “1.5.0”)

B. /home/user/hardener/CHANGELOG.md
   The first 15 lines must appear exactly as (YYYY-MM-DD to be replaced by
   real date at runtime, here assumed 2024-05-17):

   ----------------------------------------------------
   ## [1.5.0] - 2024-05-17
   ### Added
   - _None_

   ### Changed
   - _None_

   ### Fixed
   - _None_

   ## [1.4.2] - 2023-08-07
   ### Added
   - Initial iptables hardening rules.
   ...
   ----------------------------------------------------
   The rest of the pre-existing changelog follows unchanged.

C. /home/user/hardener/bump.log
   • Exists.
   • UTF-8 plain text.
   • Contains between 5 and 10 non-blank lines.
   • Each line is a shell command that was plausibly executed during the bump,
     e.g. “cd /home/user/hardener”, “old=$(cat version.txt)”, etc.
   • The lines appear in chronological order of execution.

No other files may be created inside /home/user/hardener.  
Permissions inherit defaults (rw for user).
